author: Modet Renee
comment: Ana Abiyedh Passion is an absolute gem! From the first spray, I was captivated by its beautiful blend of fruity sweetness and warm vanilla notes. It smells luxurious, long-lasting, and far more expensive than its price point. To me, it has a vibe similar to Power of You, but even better. The fragrance is smoother, richer, and develops beautifully throughout the day. I constantly receive compliments whenever I wear it. If you love feminine, sweet, and sophisticated scents with excellent performance, Ana Abiyedh Passion is definitely worth adding to your collection. Lattafa truly created something special with this one.

author: Danuta Radzik
comment: ​If you are looking for a lush, romantic garden or a fresh bouquet of flowers, keep walking. Despite the name Queen of Roses, this fragrance is a massive curveball. The name is almost entirely misleading. There is no trace of a floral "bouquet" here. Instead of soft petals, the dominant note is a heavy, rugged leather. It is sharp, smoky, and earthy, completely bypassing the delicate sweetness one would expect from a rose-centric perfume. ​This is not a traditional feminine fragrance. It leans heavily toward the unisex category, bordering on masculine. It lacks the "pretty" or airy qualities usually marketed to women, making it much more suitable for: Fans of bold, "tough" scents. Those who prefer a very masculine-leaning feminine vibe. Anyone who enjoys the smell of a leather workshop over a flower shop.​The branding is a total mismatch for the juice inside. It’s a sophisticated leather scent, but the "Rose" in the title feels like a ghost. If you want to smell like high-end leather gear, you'll love it; if you want to smell like a queen’s rose garden, you’ll be disappointed.

author: Ozlem Albert
comment: All fragrances will remind you of something or someone. Most well made fragrances, whether musk, floral, sweet, woodsy- all will undoubtedly remind you of something or someone. With that said, this fragrance only reminded me of a airwick oil plug in that I would never buy again. I wish I could return it for a different fragrance from this company. Disappointment doesn’t match the degree of displeasure I felt them moment I sprayed this on my skin. I know this company has better fragrances. I chose the mistake that should have never been brought to production.

comment: Most people relate this fragrance to YSL EDP, I do not get that, it’s OSS in the same-ish wheelhouse. It is fresh, sweet, clean and powerful, if you approach this as a stand alone scent in a blue style fragrance but more cooler weather leaning, it does not disappoint. Very appealing and well done!

author: Perfume Diva
comment: I adore Lattafa perfumes -- they are rich, elegant, sophisticated, luxurious, smell expensive (but aren't), unique -- plus the gorgeous packaging and bottles. I don't know how they do it, but I'm so glad they do.I added this set to my ever-growing collection (I'm addicted). As always I let the scent macerate for about 6 weeks-2 months in the cool dark. When I opened it this morning I was instantly captivated and truly blown away by the scent. It's like nothing else I have and I'm so glad I bought it.  All my purchases have been blind buys based on the notes/ reviews and I've never been disappointed.I have 3 words of advice  -- "Buy this now." The gift set is an amazing value and the hair mist takes the scent experience to the next level. The scent is truly unisex and you will adore it.
